items.title: One commit per coherent change
items.scenario: You change the prompt template AND fix a typo in the README in the same session.
items.explanation: Stage and commit them separately: `git add prompts/qa.md && git commit -m 'Tighten QA prompt'` then `git add README.md && git commit -m 'Fix typo'`. Reviewers and `git bisect` thank you.

items.title: One branch per experiment
items.scenario: You're trying three reranker strategies and want to keep `main` clean while you iterate.
items.explanation: Cut `git switch -c rerank/cross-encoder`, `rerank/llm-judge`, `rerank/heuristic` off `main`. Run evals on each branch; merge whichever wins.

items.title: Reproducing an eval result
items.scenario: Your dashboard reports an eval score from two weeks ago and the team wants to reproduce it.
items.explanation: If the eval log saved the commit SHA, `git switch <sha>` puts the entire repo back into that exact state — prompts, configs, evaluator code.

items.title: Branch fan-out
items.scenario: Three teammates take the same prototype in three directions for an experiment week.
items.explanation: Each cuts a branch off the same commit. Branches are O(1) — there's no copy cost. Whichever one we land merges into `main`; the others stay as preserved history.
